precision.description: When KRITE flags content as 'needs revision,' human reviewer agrees >= 90% of the time. (Low false-positive flags.)
precision.evidence_required: Marc's audit log of last 10 KRITE flags + agree/disagree verdicts

recall.description: When content is actually broken (caught later by Marc, prospect, or downstream agent), KRITE caught it >= 90% of the time. (Low false-negatives.)

scoring.90_100: Catch rate >= 90% (only 1 in 10 issues slip past KRITE).
scoring.0_49: Catch rate < 60% — KRITE is missing real issues.

recall.evidence_required: Log of post-release issues + whether KRITE flagged at time of review

false_positive_rate.evidence_required: Same audit log; specifically count of 'KRITE flagged but Marc disagreed'

turnaround_time.description: Median KRITE review completion time < 30 sec.

actionable_feedback.description: Each flagged issue includes specific line + suggested fix (not just 'needs revision').

scoring.90_100: Every flag includes line reference + concrete suggestion.

actionable_feedback.evidence_required: Sample 10 random KRITE verdicts; count actionability

ground_truth_signals: Marc agreement on flagged items (binary per item)
Post-release issues caught vs missed (downstream feedback)
self_validation_note: KRITE cannot validate its own self-improvement proposals. Marc validates directly per scorecard system spec.
